content.full_text:
ARTER, HADDEN,
LAWLER, FELIX & HALL

700 South Flower Street
Los Angeles, CA 90017
Telephone 213/629-9300
Telecopy 213/617-9255

Writers Direct Dial Number:

(213) 629-9328


September 28, 1990


VIA FAX

Ms. Luisa Menchaca
Staff Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, CA

Dear Ms. Menchaca:

This confirms our conversation on September 28, 1990 at approximately 3:00 p.m. in which you affirmed the legality of my contribution of $5,000 to Feinstein for Governor, on the basis of the fact that no stay has yet been obtained to the Order o f the District Court overturning Proposition 72. This appeared to me to be obvious but I called to make sure I was doing the right thing.

Very truly yours,

Darrell J. Salomon

DJS:jfs


IN CLEVELAND

ARTER & HADDEN
1100 Huntington Building
Cleveland, Ohio 44115
216/696-1100

IN COLUMBUS

ARTER & HADDEN
10 West Broad Street
Columbus, Ohio 43215
614/221-3133

IN DALLAS

ARTER & HADDEN
1717 Main Street, Suite 4100
Dallas, Texas 75201
214/761-2100

IN WASHINGTON D.C.

ARTER & HADDEN
1919 Pennsylvania Avenue, N.W.
Washington, D.C. 20006
202/775-7100

sections.question_synthetic: Is a $5,000 contribution to Feinstein for Governor legal given the status of Proposition 72 and a District Court order?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request/inquiry letter written to FPPC staff, not an FPPC advice letter response. The letter confirms a phone conversation with Ms. Luisa Menchaca (FPPC Staff Counsel) rather than presenting a formal request. No formal question or conclusion sections are present. Document describes affirmation of contribution legality during the call.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is a $5,000 contribution to Feinstein for Governor legal given the status of Proposition 72 and a District Court order?
embedding.first_500_words: September 28, 1990 VIA FAX Ms. Luisa Menchaca Staff Counsel Fair Political Practices Commission 428 "J" Street, Suite 800 Sacramento, CA Dear Ms. Menchaca: This confirms our conversation on September 28, 1990 at approximately 3:00 p.m. in which you affirmed the legality of my contribution of $5,000 to Feinstein for Governor, on the basis of the fact that no stay has yet been obtained to the Order of the District Court overturning Proposition 72. This appeared to me to be obvious but I called to make sure I was doing the right thing. Very truly yours, Darrell J. Salomon DJS:jfs
embedding.summary: A contributor seeks confirmation from FPPC staff counsel that a $5,000 contribution to Feinstein for Governor is legal based on the lack of a stay on a District Court order overturning Proposition 72.
